what_it_does: A local HTTP server that serves a mobile-first PWA for reviewing cc_board documents and chatting with Vi. It renders ordered document blocks, lets the operator tap paragraphs/sections/the whole document to add anchored comments stored via cc_board, and provides a real-time chat view using Server-Sent Events, image attachments, browser speech recognition, and a fabric push receiver.
observations: The file contains two complete inline HTML/CSS/JS templates: PAGE for document review and CHAT_PAGE for messaging.
It registers the running app as a channel member ('tim') by writing a JSON file so the fabric can push replies to it.
Comments are stored with an envelope syntax '[scale] re: «quote»' and then parsed so the UI shows only a scale chip and human text.
A single-pass _build_index replaces per-block reverse traversal to avoid timeouts on large boards.
It serves PWA assets, a web manifest, and DNA-style bronze SVG icons with no emojis.
Real-time updates use an SSE /chat-stream endpoint that also broadcasts a per-process VERSION for auto-reload on restart.
The chat composer includes browser SpeechRecognition (webkit) for dictation and supports image attachments.
The file is truncated mid-implementation of the /comment POST handler.
standouts: Real-time bidirectional surface via SSE plus a fabric push receiver on the same port.
Granular tap-to-comment on paragraphs, headings, bullets, blockquotes, sections, or the whole document.
PWA-capable packaging with manifest, theme color, icons, and safe-area handling.
Auto-reload on server restart using a timestamp-based VERSION and sessionStorage 'app_updated' animation.
conventions: Global constants and singletons (STORE, VERSION, OPERATOR_HANDLE) defined at the top.
sys.path insertion to import runtime modules from the repo root.
Inline string templates with placeholder replacement after the template is defined.
Broad exception catching (BrokenPipeError, ConnectionResetError, generic Exception) to keep the server from crashing on client or downstream errors.
DNA/bronze/goldwash visual language expressed entirely in CSS and SVG stroke icons.
concerns: The file is truncated before the full /comment, /comment-edit, and /comment-delete handlers are visible.
No authentication on the POST / injection endpoint; security relies entirely on 127.0.0.1 + tailnet exposure.
VERSION changes on every process restart, so frequent restarts would force client reloads.
The markdown parser is regex-based and may mishandle nested or edge-case formatting.
LEAD_TARGET_FILE content is used as a send target without visible validation.
Comment edit/delete event listeners are bound only to initially rendered elements; dynamic content would need delegation.
notes: A tailnet-local PWA server that turns cc_board documents into a phone review surface and a two-way chat with Vi, using SSE and the cc_board runtime.
questions: What is the exact schema for cc_board items and links used by _build_index and _thread?
How is the lead target file populated and rotated, and is its content validated before cc.send?
What do the truncated /comment-edit and /comment-delete endpoints return on success or failure?
How does the channel transport (cc.send / route_reply) deliver messages back to this server's POST / endpoint?
Is there any plan for authentication beyond the tailnet/localhost boundary?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: a local, phone-reachable review surface for board documents with granular commenting, document switching, PWA support, and a real-time chat bridge.
intention_signals: Make board-document review feel like a messaging app on a phone, preserve the exact context of every comment as an anchor, and let the operator chat with the lead/Vi without polling or manual refresh.
novelty: Combines a PWA document review surface with per-paragraph anchored comments, SSE-driven auto-reload on server restart, and in-browser speech recognition for chat in a single local Python server.
contribution: A self-contained, installable web UI that surfaces cc_board documents and messages, captures structured annotations with context anchors, and bridges operator input back into the channel via cc_board and cc_channels.
summary_for_embedding: A local Python HTTP server that renders cc_board documents as a mobile PWA for granular commenting and provides a real-time chat surface with SSE, image attachments, and voice input.
intention_for_embedding: To give the operator a phone-reachable, app-like review and chat interface for board documents and channel messages.

symbols.does: Pushes a JSON payload to every connected SSE client queue.
symbols.description: Iterates SSE_CLIENTS under a lock and enqueues the payload; the /chat-stream handler later dequeues each payload and writes it as a server-sent event.

symbols.does: Builds the SSE/chat display payload from a stored message record.
symbols.description: Labels the author as 'You' or 'Vi', extracts the message body, and collects attachment image URLs from links of kind 'attachment'.

symbols.does: Writes a channel registration file for the operator app.
symbols.description: Persists handle 'tim', port, pid, transport, and start time to .data/channels/tim.json so the fabric can route replies to this server.

symbols.does: Escapes HTML and applies lightweight inline markdown formatting.
symbols.description: HTML-escapes the input, then converts backticks to <code>, double asterisks to <strong>, and single asterisks to <em>.

symbols.does: Converts a markdown-like text block into HTML.
symbols.description: Line-oriented parser handling headings, horizontal rules, blockquotes, unordered and ordered lists with continuation folding, and paragraphs; relies on _inline for inline formatting.

symbols.does: Splits a stored comment body into scale, quote, and human text.
symbols.description: Parses the envelope syntax '[scale] re: «quote» text' so the UI can show a clean scale chip and the comment text without exposing the raw anchor.

symbols.does: Renders a nested comment thread as HTML.
symbols.description: Recursively builds <div class='comments'> containing comment cards with author chips, edit/delete actions, bodies, attached images, and nested replies.

symbols.does: Returns a short display label from a document title.
symbols.description: Takes the portion before an em dash and truncates the result to 32 characters for the top-bar title.

symbols.does: Scans the board once and builds lookup structures.
symbols.description: Returns a tuple of items by address, a reverse edge map keyed by (target, kind), and the list of documents in the configured channel sorted by creation time.

symbols.does: Builds the top-level comment thread for a block address.
symbols.description: Uses the reverse edge map to find items linked with 'commented_on' to the address, then recursively nests 'reply_to' links via the inner nest function.

symbols.does: Recursively collects replies to a comment address.
symbols.description: Returns a list of comment dicts with their own nested replies by following 'reply_to' edges in the reverse map.

symbols.does: Renders the hamburger drawer navigation links.
symbols.description: Builds HTML for the chat link and a list of document links, marking the current document with a distinct style.

symbols.does: Renders the full document review HTML page for a doc id.
symbols.description: Builds the board index, resolves the document and its ordered blocks, renders each block with its key, title, body, and comment thread, and fills the PAGE template.

symbols.does: Counts comments recursively in a thread.
symbols.description: Sums one per comment plus the recursive count of all replies, used to populate the comment-dot badge.

symbols.does: Renders the chat history page.
symbols.description: Loads channel messages, sorts them by creation time, and fills the CHAT_PAGE template with message bubbles and attached images.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler for the review server.
symbols.description: Subclass of BaseHTTPRequestHandler serving static assets, images, the PWA manifest, the SSE stream, document pages, the chat page, and POST endpoints for comments and chat.

symbols.does: Silences default request logging.
symbols.description: Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to do nothing, keeping server output quiet.

symbols.does: Writes an HTTP response with status, headers, and body.
symbols.description: Encodes string bodies as UTF-8, sets Content-Type and Content-Length, and swallows broken-pipe errors on write.

symbols.does: Serves a static file from disk.
symbols.description: Opens the given path in binary mode and sends it with the supplied MIME type, or returns 404 on failure.

symbols.does: Routes GET requests to assets, images, manifest, SSE, chat, and document pages.
symbols.description: Handles icon files, image blobs via cc_images, the web manifest, the /chat-stream SSE endpoint, /chat history, and /doc/<id> or the default document rendering.

symbols.does: Routes POST endpoints for fabric injection, chat, and comments.
symbols.description: Handles the root path as a fabric push receiver for messages, /chat-send for operator chat messages with optional images, and /comment (truncated) for storing annotations; returns JSON responses.

symbols.does: Parses arguments and starts the HTTP server.
symbols.description: Reads --port and --author arguments, registers the operator, and runs ThreadingHTTPServer bound to 127.0.0.1 on the chosen port.
